Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America For Its Failure To Comply With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 871.26, Subd. (C) Initial Deposition Of Pmq Witness; Memorandum Of Points And Auth
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Phil A Thomas, Esq., Knight Law Group, LLP, 310-552-2250 philt@knightlaw.com
Attorney for Plaintiffs, Sida Wang Faggioli Nathaniel Faggioli

SARAH CARLSON LAMBERT, ESQ., GORDON REES SCULLY MANSUKHANI, LLP (415) 986-5900 slambert@grsm.com Attorneys for Defendant JAGUAR LAND ROVER NORTH AMERICA, LLC

Hearing is held. ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFFS SIDA FAGGIOLI, NATHANIEL FAGGIOLI'S Motion For Sanctions Against Defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America For Its Failure To Comply With Code Of Civil Procedure Section 871.26, Subd. (C) Initial Deposition Of Pmq Witness

Plaintiffs' motion for sanctions against defendant Jaguar Land Rover North America for failure to comply with CCP 871.26(c) is denied. Jaguar has shown good cause within the meaning of CCP 871.26(j) for its failure to produce a PMQ witness within 120 days due to its reasonable request for the entry of a protective order prior to the deposition. See February 9, 2026 order at 2:24-2-3:4 (good cause to seek entry of a protective order before a CCP 871.26 deposition).

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling.

Visiting Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/HEK)
